doctor.summary: Inspect local Modal package and credential setup, with beginner next steps.

arguments.port: Port declared with --encrypted-port or --unencrypted-port at sandbox creation.

options.requires --sandbox-id or --sandbox-name: Attach to a sandbox created with a declared port.

domain.summary: Print the public URL for a declared sandbox port.

arguments.local_path: Destination path on the local machine.
arguments.remote_path: Relative workspace path or absolute sandbox path.

download.summary: Copy a sandbox file or directory to the local machine.

options.global --dry: Alias for this command when no subcommand is provided.

dry.summary: List safe discovery commands that do not create Modal resources.

ls.summary: List direct children of a sandbox directory.

arguments.path: Relative workspace path or absolute sandbox path.

options.--no-parents: Do not create missing parent directories.

mkdir.summary: Create a directory inside the sandbox workspace.

options.--run: Create a short-lived Modal Sandbox and run the quickstart Python command.
options.global creation options: With --run, supports --name, --tag, --image, --runtime, --workspace, --workspace-volume, --volume, --env, network policy, resources, ports, and timeout flags.

quickstart.summary: Preview or run the first beginner sandbox command.

arguments.path: Relative workspace path or absolute sandbox path.

read.summary: Read UTF-8 text from a file inside the sandbox workspace.

arguments.path: Relative workspace path or absolute sandbox path.

rm.summary: Remove a file or directory inside the sandbox workspace.

options.--cwd: Working directory inside the sandbox. Relative paths resolve inside the workspace.
options.--use-command-exit-code: Return the sandbox command exit code as the CLI exit code.
options.global --max-output-bytes: Maximum captured bytes for stdout and stderr separately. Use 0 to capture no bytes.

run.summary: Run a shell command inside a Modal sandbox.

arguments.args: Arguments passed to the executable without shell parsing.

options.--cwd: Working directory inside the sandbox. Relative paths resolve inside the workspace.
options.--use-command-exit-code: Return the sandbox command exit code as the CLI exit code.
options.global --max-output-bytes: Maximum captured bytes for stdout and stderr separately. Use 0 to capture no bytes.

run-command.summary: Run an argv-style command without shell wrapping.

options.requires --workspace-volume: Snapshot checkpoints are backed by the workspace Modal volume.

snapshot.summary: Create a volume-backed workspace snapshot checkpoint.

options.global creation options: Supports --name, --tag, --image, --runtime, --workspace, --workspace-volume, --volume, --env, network policy, resources, ports, and timeout flags.

start.summary: Create a Modal sandbox, print its ID, and leave it running.

arguments.sandbox_id: Modal sandbox object ID. Can also be passed with --sandbox-id.

stop.summary: Terminate a running Modal sandbox by ID or name.

arguments.remote_path: Relative workspace path or absolute sandbox path.

upload.summary: Copy a local file or directory into the sandbox.

arguments.path: Relative workspace path or absolute sandbox path.

options.--content-file: Local UTF-8 text file to read and write.
options.--stdin: Read UTF-8 text from standard input.

write.summary: Write UTF-8 text to a file inside the sandbox workspace.

description: CLI for running commands and file workflows inside Modal Sandboxes.

global_options.--allow-cidr CIDR: Allow sandbox outbound network access to a CIDR range. Repeatable.
global_options.--allow-domain DOMAIN: Allow sandbox outbound network access to a domain. Repeatable.
global_options.--allow-inbound-cidr CIDR: Allow inbound tunnel/connect-token access from a CIDR range. Repeatable.
global_options.--app-name: Modal app name used for sandbox creation.
global_options.--block-network: Block outbound network access from the sandbox.
global_options.--cpu: CPU request passed through to Modal.
global_options.--encrypted-port: Expose an HTTPS Modal tunnel for the given port. Repeatable.
global_options.--env KEY=VALUE: Environment variable passed to the sandbox. Repeatable.
global_options.--gpu: GPU request passed through to Modal.
global_options.--image: Registry image tag or supported alias passed to Modal.
global_options.--max-output-bytes: Maximum captured bytes for stdout and stderr separately. Defaults to 10485760; use 0 to capture no bytes.
global_options.--memory: Memory request in MiB passed through to Modal.
global_options.--name: Name assigned to a newly created sandbox. Unique within the app while running.
global_options.--region: Region preference passed through to Modal.
global_options.--runtime: Vercel-style runtime alias. Supported values: python3.13, node24, node22.
global_options.--sandbox-id: Attach to an existing Modal sandbox by ID instead of creating one.
global_options.--sandbox-name: Attach to an existing running Modal sandbox by name instead of creating one.
global_options.--sandbox-timeout: Modal sandbox lifetime timeout in seconds.
global_options.--tag KEY=VALUE: Tag assigned to a newly created sandbox. Repeatable.
global_options.--timeout: Command timeout in seconds for run.
global_options.--unencrypted-port: Expose a TCP Modal tunnel for the given port. Repeatable.
global_options.--volume NAME:/mount: Modal volume name and absolute sandbox mount path. Repeatable.
global_options.--workspace: Default sandbox directory for relative paths.
global_options.--workspace-volume: Modal volume name mounted at the workspace path.

golden_workflows.purpose: Inspect local readiness before creating Modal resources.
golden_workflows.success_signal: quickstart reports ready_to_run or gives setup next steps.

golden_workflows.purpose: Create one short-lived sandbox and verify command execution.
golden_workflows.success_signal: command JSON has exit_code 0 and expected stdout.

golden_workflows.purpose: Preserve files across separate CLI calls using a Modal workspace volume.
golden_workflows.success_signal: read returns the file content and snapshot names the workspace volume.

golden_workflows.purpose: Reuse one live sandbox for iterative work, then terminate it.
golden_workflows.success_signal: start returns sandbox_id and sandbox_name; stop returns status terminated.

lifecycle.cidr_allowlists: Use --allow-cidr CIDR for outbound IP ranges and --allow-inbound-cidr CIDR for inbound tunnel/connect-token ranges.
lifecycle.domain_allowlist: Use --allow-domain DOMAIN to restrict sandbox outbound network access to listed domains.
lifecycle.long_lived_cli_workflow: Use start to create a sandbox, --sandbox-id to reuse it, and stop to terminate it.
lifecycle.named_sandboxes: Use --name NAME when starting a sandbox and --sandbox-name NAME to attach to the currently running named sandbox.
lifecycle.persistent_files: Use --workspace-volume to preserve files across separate CLI commands.
lifecycle.preflight_validation: Invalid lifecycle combinations and global configuration are rejected before sandbox creation.
lifecycle.volume_mounts: Use --volume NAME:/mount to mount additional Modal volumes at absolute sandbox paths.

name: sandbox

path_rules.absolute_paths: Used as absolute paths inside the sandbox.
path_rules.workspace_escape: Relative paths using '..' cannot escape the workspace.

recommended_first_commands.purpose: List safe discovery commands before taking action.
Inspect the CLI contract before taking action.
Check local Modal package and credential readiness.

Create a short-lived Modal Sandbox and run a tiny Python command.
